name: Salto del Fraile y el castillo de Castro Ferral - circular por el P.N. de Despeñaperros
region_intro: Mountain biking around Santa Elena offers a diverse landscape characterized by the rugged terrain of Despeñaperros Natural Park. The region features deep gorges, Mediterranean forests, and rocky outcrops, providing varied challenges for mountain bikers. Trails often navigate through dense woodlands and along riverbeds, with significant elevation changes. This area provides a natural setting for adventurous rides amidst its unique geological formations. Best mountain bike trails around Santa Elena The most popular mountain bike trail is Salto del Fraile y el castillo de Castro Ferral - circular por el P.N. de Despeñaperros, an 18.6 miles (30.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 0 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural park, passing by historical sites and scenic viewpoints. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Empedraillo – La Aliseda Recreation Area loop from Santa Elena, a difficult 43.3 miles (69.7 km) path. This extensive loop offers a challenging ride through varied terrain, including climbs and descents within the natural park. Local mountain bikers also love the Miranda del Rey – La Aliseda Recreation Area loop from Miranda del Rey, a 25.8 miles (41.5 km) trail leading through the Despeñaperros Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Mountain biking around Santa Elena is defined by its deep gorges, Mediterranean forests, and rocky terrain. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Santa Elena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Santa Elena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Santa Elena?
A: There are 11 mountain bike trails around Santa Elena listed on komoot, offering a diverse range of experiences within the rugged terrain of Despeñaperros Natural Park.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Santa Elena?
A: Yes, Santa Elena offers trails for various skill levels. There are 2 easy mountain bike routes available, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Santa Elena?
A: Santa Elena's Mediterranean climate generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. However, spring and autumn typically provide the most pleasant temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any loop mountain bike trails in Santa Elena?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Santa Elena are loops. For example, you can explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990323650/salto-del-fraile-y-el-castillo-de-castro-ferral-circular-por-el-p-n-de-despenaperros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Fraile y el castillo de Castro Ferral - circular por el P.N. de Despeñaperros&lt;/a&gt; or the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31863797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Empedraillo – La Aliseda Recreation Area loop from Santa Elena&lt;/a&gt;, both offering circular routes through the natural park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Santa Elena?
A: The terrain around Santa Elena is characterized by the rugged landscape of Despeñaperros Natural Park. You can expect deep gorges, Mediterranean forests, and rocky outcrops, with significant elevation changes that provide varied challenges for mountain bikers.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region is known for its dramatic landscapes. You can find stunning views at highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Despeñaperros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4215341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Órganos&lt;/a&gt;, which are often accessible from or near the trails.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Santa Elena?
A: The mountain bike trails in Santa Elena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the challenging and varied terrain, the natural beauty of Despeñaperros Natural Park, and the opportunity to explore historical sites along the routes.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions to see while mountain biking?
A: Yes, the area around Santa Elena is rich in history and natural beauty. Along the trails, you might encounter sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4072358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Empedraillo&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4215375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Castillo de Castro Ferral&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4072310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Américas Farmhouse and Moorish Oven&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are permits required for mountain biking in Despeñaperros Natural Park?
A: While general access to Despeñaperros Natural Park for activities like mountain biking is usually permitted, it's always advisable to check with the park's official administration for any specific regulations or permit requirements for certain areas or activities, especially for larger groups or organized events.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Santa Elena?
A: Many trailheads and recreation areas within Despeñaperros Natural Park, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2638820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Aliseda Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt;, offer designated parking facilities. It's recommended to check the starting points of specific routes on komoot for detailed parking information.

Q: Are there options for moderate difficulty mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, Santa Elena offers a good selection of moderate trails. There are 4 moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24524616" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miranda del Rey – La Aliseda Recreation Area loop from Miranda del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a balanced challenge for intermediate riders.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to eat near the mountain bike trails?
A: Santa Elena and its surrounding villages offer various amenities. While specific cafes directly on remote trails might be limited, you can typically find places to eat and drink in the towns and villages bordering Despeñaperros Natural Park, such as Santa Elena itself or Miranda del Rey.
tips:
It is an emblematic place with a spectacular waterfall from the mountain.
Now it usually carries less water but you can see the fall of the water from the heights.
It has several wells that carry the liquid element to inhabited places, since it seems to be an area with several springs.

It is a short journey along a paved road that treasures a great history as it is attributed an antiquity that dates back to Roman times. It is formed by a careful network of stones nailed to the ground as a firm that would ensure the adherence of the cavalry, since it is located on an old Roman road.
The curious thing is that it is in the middle of nowhere and perhaps at some point it was much larger than what we can now enjoy, because it only measures a couple of hundred meters.
In any case, it is a vestige of the past that is well worth observing, preserving and stepping on, since very relevant historical events took place on those same stones, such as the battle of Las Navas de Tolosa that took place in these places back in 1212.

These remains correspond to an old fortress perched on a promontory known as Castro Ferral.
This castle was demolished (it seems to be by the Castilian armies, during the battle of Las Navas de Tolosa that took place on July 16, 1212, to prevent it from falling into Muslim hands.
It has been like this for several centuries as a mute witness to the glorious historical past. Stepping on that terrain and touching those nearby rocks take us back to important moments in our history.
To climb it is necessary to make an effort, both on one side and on the other, by a steep firewall, although the reward for both the place and the views are well worth it.

waypoints.name: Salto del Fraile Waterfall and Spring
